title: At the Fruit Shop
summary: A customer visits a fruit shop and buys some fruit from the shopkeeper.

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: Good morning! Welcome to my shop. What would you like today?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Good morning! I would like some apples, please.

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: Of course. How many apples do you want?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: I want six apples, please. Are they fresh?

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: Yes, they are very fresh. They came in this morning.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Great! And do you have any bananas?

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: Yes, we have yellow bananas over here. They are very ripe and sweet.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: I will take a bunch of bananas too, please.

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: No problem. Is there anything else you need?

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: I am sorry, we do not have strawberries today. We will have them tomorrow.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: That is okay. Just the apples and bananas then.

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: That will be five dollars, please.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Here you are. Thank you very much!

body.speaker: Shopkeeper
body.text: Thank you! Have a nice day!

annotations.exampleEn: The bananas are ripe and ready to eat.
She bought a bunch of grapes at the market.
Is there anything else I can help you with?
I am sorry, we are closed today.
That is okay, I will come back later.
The cashier smiled and said, 'Have a nice day!'
The bananas are over here, near the entrance.
That will be ten dollars, please.

grammarNotes.title: Would like for polite requests
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Would like' dùng để yêu cầu một cách lịch sự thay vì 'want': 'I would like some apples, please.' Đây là cách nói lịch sự hơn 'I want apples.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Would like' is used for polite requests instead of 'want': 'I would like some apples, please.' It is more polite than saying 'I want apples.'

grammarNotes.title: Do you have any...?
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Do you have any...?' dùng để hỏi xem có gì đó không, thường dùng trong cửa hàng: 'Do you have any bananas?' 'Any' dùng với danh từ số nhiều không đếm được trong câu hỏi và phủ định.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Do you have any...?' is used to ask whether something is available, common in shops: 'Do you have any bananas?' 'Any' is used with plural or uncountable nouns in questions and negatives.

translationVi: Người bán hàng: Chào buổi sáng! Chào mừng đến cửa hàng của tôi. Hôm nay bạn muốn gì? Khách hàng: Chào buổi sáng! Tôi muốn mua một ít táo, làm ơn. Người bán hàng: Tất nhiên rồi. Bạn muốn mấy quả táo? Khách hàng: Tôi muốn sáu quả táo, làm ơn. Chúng có tươi không? Người bán hàng: Vâng, chúng rất tươi. Chúng vừa vào sáng nay. Khách hàng: Tuyệt! Và bạn có chuối không? Người bán hàng: Vâng, chúng tôi có chuối vàng ở đây. Chúng rất chín và ngọt. Khách hàng: Tôi sẽ lấy một buồng chuối nữa, làm ơn. Người bán hàng: Không vấn đề gì. Bạn có cần gì thêm không? Khách hàng: Hôm nay bạn có dâu tây không? Người bán hàng: Tôi xin lỗi, hôm nay chúng tôi không có dâu tây. Ngày mai chúng tôi sẽ có. Khách hàng: Không sao. Vậy thì chỉ táo và chuối thôi. Người bán hàng: Tổng cộng là năm đô la, làm ơn. Khách hàng: Của bạn đây. Cảm ơn rất nhiều! Người bán hàng: Cảm ơn bạn! Chúc một ngày tốt lành!

questions.prompt: What does the customer want to buy first?
questions.hint: Listen to the customer's first request.

questions.hint: Find the line where the customer gives a number.

questions.prompt: When did the apples arrive at the shop?
questions.hint: Look for the shopkeeper's answer about freshness.

questions.hint: Find the shopkeeper's sentence about bananas.

questions.explanation: 'I will take a bunch of bananas too, please.'
questions.hint: Listen for what the customer adds to their order.

questions.hint: Find the shopkeeper's reply about strawberries.

questions.hint: Look at the last line of the dialogue.

questions.hint: Find the shopkeeper's description of the bananas.

questions.prompt: What does the customer say when the shopkeeper gives the total?
questions.explanation: 'Here you are. Thank you very much!'
questions.hint: Find the customer's response to the price.

questions.prompt: What is the setting of this conversation?
questions.hint: Look at the shopkeeper's opening words.

cloze.template: Shopkeeper: Good morning! Welcome to my shop. What would you like {{b1}}? Customer: Good morning! I would like some {{b2}}, please. Shopkeeper: Of course. How {{b3}} apples do you want? Customer: I want six apples, please. Are they {{b4}}? Shopkeeper: Yes, they are very fresh. They came in this {{b5}}. Customer: Great! And do you have any {{b6}}? Shopkeeper: Yes, we have yellow bananas over here. They are very {{b7}} and sweet. Customer: I will take a {{b8}} of bananas too, please. Shopkeeper: Is there anything else you {{b9}}? Customer: Do you have {{b10}} today? Shopkeeper: I am {{b11}}, we do not have strawberries today. We will have them {{b12}}. Customer: That is okay. Just the apples and bananas then. Shopkeeper: That will be five {{b13}}, please.

blanks.explanation: 'I will take a bunch of bananas.'
'I am sorry, we do not have strawberries today.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The shopkeeper said the strawberries would arrive tomorrow. If you were the customer, would you come back the next day? What makes a fruit shop a good place to shop?
